steps.title: Find the common factor
steps.content: Look for the greatest common factor in both terms. The coefficients $5$ and $15$ share $5$, the powers $x^9$ and $x^4$ share $x^4$, and both terms include $y^3$.

steps.title: Combine the leftover terms
steps.content: Put the remaining pieces inside one set of parentheses: $$ 5x^4y^3(x^5+3) $$ This keeps both original terms when distributed back through the parentheses. The answer is C.
